action.reasoning: Observation: test crashed on TypeError (dict vs int for robot_collisions); v20 reached best-yet 63-safe-streak/112-detector in ep0 (min_d=1.12) but avg safe low and never hits 500; config thresholds confirmed exactly (500 accumulated steps in [1.0,2.0)m with success_angle=0.2618rad/15deg, facing=0.5). Earlier runs ~50-100 safe. Analysis: truncation/incomplete post-episode metrics handling is root cause of crash; follow drifts from imperfect speed-match (human ~0.25-0.35m/s), oscillation on dynamic oracle target, occasional OA interference. Reactive heuristic can reach ~0.5 follow-rate per literature but needs tighter stabilization. Root cause of limited streaks is gain/speed-matching not robust enough for persistent facing+dist. Decision: rewrite COMPLETE non-truncated test_full_episode.py + predict.py with clean v21 HeuristicPolicy (base_follow=0.28, err_gain=0.05, ang_k=-7.0, pure_rotate>0.35rad, follow on detector>0.05|dist<3.5, OA gated by detector<0.25+min_front<1.2 with left/right bias, backup<0.8, aligned_safe_vel=0.25, robust depth valid>0.1 + center bias, per-step safe/streak/max_safe/detector tracking inside loop, fixed collision check using did_collide + safe dict handling for robot_collisions, manual safe_follow_steps, full per-epi JSON with all required keys + make_serializable, abs paths+dataset override, proper Env context). Update train.py to v21. Run test_full_episode.py (5 eps) for validation of higher safe/streak before submit_eval on predict.py (dummy ckpt). Targets >150 safe in good eps for first positive nav_seek_success.
